intent: An AI-powered logistics platform that optimizes last-mile delivery by dynamically batching orders from multiple local vendors into a single courier circuit. The value proposition lies in reducing carbon footprint and driver wait times through predictive traffic analysis and real-time route re-sequencing based on order readiness status.

entities.name: OrderReadinessSignal
entities.description: An event-driven record capturing the timestamped status of order assembly from vendor POS systems, triggering route recalculation.

entities.name: DynamicRouteCircuit
entities.description: An ephemeral entity representing a clustered sequence of delivery nodes calculated via the predictive traffic analysis engine.

entities.name: CourierTelemetryStream
entities.description: A high-frequency stream of GPS coordinates and velocity data points ingested to support real-time proximity matching.

entities.name: SettlementLedger
entities.description: An immutable audit trail that manages split-payment distribution between multiple vendors and the courier following delivery confirmation.

entities.name: TrafficPredictorModel
entities.description: A feature store entity maintaining historical and real-time transit patterns used to weight route cost functions.

entities.name: VendorAvailabilityProfile
entities.description: A state-tracking entity that maintains vendor throughput capacity and operational constraints to prevent over-batching.

entities.name: DispatchAssignment
entities.description: A state machine binding a specific Courier to a DynamicRouteCircuit, managing lifecycle events from pickup acceptance to final handover.

architecture.description: The system employs an event-driven architecture using Kafka to ingest OrderReadinessSignals and CourierTelemetryStream data. Upon an order trigger, the TrafficPredictorModel analyzes the spatial density of active orders to generate a DynamicRouteCircuit. This circuit is assigned via the DispatchAssignment engine, which accounts for VendorAvailabilityProfile constraints. Once the Courier completes the circuit, the system validates the cryptographic proof of delivery (geofenced) and triggers the SettlementLedger to execute automated payout splits through the integrated payment gateway. This decoupled approach ensures that latency in vendor signals is mitigated by re-sequencing logic, while continuous stream processing maintains high-fidelity route optimization.

endpoints.summary: Create a new order readiness signal
Get audit trail for a specific dispatch assignment
Retrieve transit patterns for a specific region
Update vendor capacity and operational constraints
Update dispatch assignment status and lifecycle events
validation_rules: status must be one of [pending, active, completed]

rules.description: Prevent DispatchAssignment if VendorAvailabilityProfile indicates throughput capacity is zero.
rules.logic: IF VendorAvailabilityProfile.currentCapacity <= 0 THEN REJECT DispatchAssignment.create ELSE ALLOW

rules.description: Verify geofenced cryptographic proof before triggering SettlementLedger payout.
rules.logic: IF CourierTelemetryStream.gpsCoordinates WITHIN DynamicRouteCircuit.deliveryNodeRadius AND CryptographicProof.valid IS TRUE THEN EXECUTE SettlementLedger.payout

rules.description: Trigger route recalculation when OrderReadinessSignal deviates significantly from current DynamicRouteCircuit state.
rules.logic: IF OrderReadinessSignal.timestamp > DynamicRouteCircuit.calculationTimestamp AND TrafficPredictorModel.congestionFactor > threshold THEN TRIGGER DynamicRouteCircuit.recalculate

rules.description: Ensure DispatchAssignment state transition cannot move from 'Handover' back to 'Pickup'.
rules.logic: IF DispatchAssignment.currentState == 'Handover' AND incomingEvent == 'PickupEvent' THEN REJECT StateTransition

rules.description: Validate SettlementLedger distribution sum equals the total order value to ensure ledger integrity.
rules.logic: IF SUM(SettlementLedger.splitDistribution) != TotalOrderValue THEN TRIGGER AuditFlag(CRITICAL_MISMATCH)
